entries.title: One Sith Reject Rule of Two

content.fact: The One Sith are a large order of Sith unified under Darth Krayt, not a Rule-of-Two pair.
content.injection: In Legacy-era scenes, do not limit Sith to master/apprentice dyads; the One Sith field many titled Sith under Krayt’s ideology. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Comics full far-future era.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends far-future Legacy comics era covering Star Wars: Legacy volume 1 with Cade Skywalker, Darth Krayt's One Sith, the Fel Empire, Galactic Alliance Remnant, Jedi survivors, and Legacy volume 2 with Ania Solo and late Sith/Imperial Knight fallout. Excludes Disney canon sequel-era material and earlier post-Endor novel arcs except as historical background or future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Use as a far-future Legends branch. Do not stack as a direct sequel-state for Disney canon or for unresolved custom post-Endor branches unless the story explicitly chooses the Legacy comics trajectory.

content.fact: During Krayt’s rule, the Sith do not merely hide in shadows; they openly control the central regime.
content.injection: During Krayt’s reign, Sith can hold visible state power, command forces, and dictate policy from Coruscant.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Darth Krayt's reign.
Respect story-position bounds from Darth Krayt's Sith Empire Reign to Darth Krayt's Apparent Death.

entries.title: Fel Empire Is Not Original Empire

content.fact: The Fel Empire is an Imperial successor state with dynastic, knightly, and anti-Sith factions, not the exact Galactic Empire of Palpatine’s era.
content.injection: Do not import Palpatine-era Imperial assumptions wholesale into Fel Empire scenes; use legitimacy, loyalty, and schism. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Comics full far-future era.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Imperial Knights are Force-users sworn to the Emperor and Imperial service, distinct from Jedi doctrine and Sith allegiance.
content.injection: When Imperial Knights appear, treat them as disciplined state-aligned Force knights, not Jedi with different uniforms. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Comics full far-future era.

content.fact: Imperial Knights serve the Emperor as long as the Emperor remains aligned with the light; their duty can oppose a fallen ruler.
content.injection: Use Imperial Knight loyalty as conditional and ideological, not blind Sith-style obedience. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

content.fact: The Galactic Alliance Remnant survives as a mobile military-political force rather than a stable capital government.
content.injection: In Legacy comics after Alliance defeat, treat the Galactic Alliance Remnant as fleet-centered, resource-constrained, and legitimacy-minded rather than a stable capital government.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Alliance Remnant after defeat.
Respect story-position bounds from Galactic Alliance Defeated to Legacy War Aftermath.

content.injection: Post-Ossus, do not portray Jedi as a public temple bureaucracy; they are hunted, hidden, and rebuilding. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi survivors after Ossus.

content.fact: Cade’s crew operates through bounty hunting, piracy, smuggling, and survival economics before he re-enters galactic politics.
content.injection: In Cade outlaw scenes, use underworld motives and debts before heroic Jedi mission logic. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Empire-Jedi-Imperial Knight Entanglement in Legacy II

content.fact: Legacy II uses Imperial Knights, Jedi, Imperial authority, and Sith remnants in overlapping roles rather than clean faction boxes.
content.injection: In Legacy II, let Imperial and Jedi-aligned forces overlap tactically while preserving doctrinal differences.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.
Respect story-position bounds from Ania Solo Era Opens to Legacy II Resolution.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
